steps.title: Identify Roots of Function g(x)
steps.content: Given the function $ g(x) = x(x - 2)(x + 6)^2 $, the roots occur where each factor equals 0. These roots are $ x = 0 $, $ x = 2 $, and $ x = -6 $ (with multiplicity 2). So, $ g(x) = 0 $ implies $ x $ must be one of these values.

steps.title: Substitute $7 - w$ Into the Roots
steps.content:
Since $ g(7 - w) = 0 $, $ 7 - w $ must equal one of the roots of $ g(x) $.

Set $ 7 - w $ equal to each root: $$7 - w = 0 \quad 7 - w = 2 \quad 7 - w = -6$$ Solving each gives $ w = 7 $, $ w = 5 $, and $ w = 13 $.

The double root at $ x = -6 $ yields only one unique $ w $ value (13) here, as both factors produce the same equation.

steps.title: Sum the Unique Values of $w$
steps.content: The distinct solutions for $ w $ are $ 7 $, $ 5 $, and $ 13 $. Adding these values: $$7 + 5 + 13 = 25$$ The answer is 25.
